purpose: Section-specific revise prompt for revising a draft statement field that failed its rubric. Composed at runtime with system_prompts/revise.json.
system_addendum:
SECTION YOU ARE REVISING: statement

The statement is { title: string, description: string }. The previous draft failed at least one dimension of the V1 statement rubric. Your revision must address every failing dimension while preserving every passing one.

DIMENSION-BY-DIMENSION REVISION GUIDANCE

If is_prescriptive failed (≤ 1):
  - The title is a noun phrase or descriptive label. Convert to an imperative verb phrase. Examples of imperative openings that have worked in the catalogue: 'Maintain…', 'Refresh…', 'Stratify…', 'Implement…', 'Monitor…'. Avoid weak verbs (have, support, ensure) — pick a verb that names the discipline.
  - Description should also use shall/must language, not aspirational language ('the harness should ideally…').

If derives_from_aws_verbatim failed (≤ 1):
  - Re-read the AWS step's verbatim text. List the load-bearing nouns and verbs. The revision's title and description should contain (or have catalogue-specific concretisations of) those terms.
  - If your draft uses a term that is not in the AWS step (e.g. 'verification chain', 'soft synthesis'), ask whether that concept is actually in the AWS step or whether you imported it from a theory of the failure mode. Remove imported theory.

If names_artefact_and_enforcement failed (≤ 1):
  - The statement (title + description together) must answer 'what must exist?' and 'what is checked?'. If only the artefact is named (the harness exists), add the check (committed to repository, runnable by anyone, gates production). If only the check is named (a gate must fail), add the artefact.
  - The description carries the path / location / shape of the artefact. The title carries the discipline.

If scope_match failed (≤ 1):
  - The justification names the specific scope leak. If it says 'absorbs sibling step N', remove the content that belongs to step N. If it says 'invents a failure mode', remove the architectural theory and re-anchor on what the AWS step actually says.
  - Check the broader BP body and sibling principles: does any sibling already cover the leaked scope? If yes, remove from this draft.

If parallel_form_with_siblings failed (≤ 1):
  - Compare your draft's title side-by-side with the sibling titles supplied. Are they all imperative? Are they similar length? Do they share vocabulary (the harness, the deployment baseline, the workload repository)?
  - Match the form. Do not invent new vocabulary if a sibling has already established the term.

WHAT TO PRESERVE

The rubric scores tell you which dimensions passed. Preserve those. The most common revision mistake is to over-rewrite and accidentally regress a passing dimension while fixing a failing one. If the title scored 3 on is_prescriptive (good imperative verb) and 1 on scope_match (absorbs sibling scope), the revised title should keep the imperative form and trim the scope.
user_template:
The previous statement draft failed its rubric. Revise it.

<principle_id>{{ principle_id }}</principle_id>

<aws_anchor>
  <best_practice>{{ aws_bp }}</best_practice>
  <implementation_step>
    <number>{{ aws_step_number }}</number>
    <title>{{ aws_step_title }}</title>
    <verbatim_text>{{ aws_step_verbatim }}</verbatim_text>
  </implementation_step>
</aws_anchor>

<siblings_under_same_bp>
{{ sibling_statements }}
</siblings_under_same_bp>

<failing_draft>
  <title>{{ candidate_title }}</title>
  <description>{{ candidate_description }}</description>
</failing_draft>

<rubric_scores>
  <is_prescriptive score="{{ score_is_prescriptive }}">{{ justification_is_prescriptive }}</is_prescriptive>
  <derives_from_aws_verbatim score="{{ score_derives_from_aws_verbatim }}">{{ justification_derives_from_aws_verbatim }}</derives_from_aws_verbatim>
  <names_artefact_and_enforcement score="{{ score_names_artefact_and_enforcement }}">{{ justification_names_artefact_and_enforcement }}</names_artefact_and_enforcement>
  <scope_match score="{{ score_scope_match }}">{{ justification_scope_match }}</scope_match>
  <parallel_form_with_siblings score="{{ score_parallel_form_with_siblings }}">{{ justification_parallel_form_with_siblings }}</parallel_form_with_siblings>
</rubric_scores>

Return the JSON object specified in the output contract — same shape as the generate output, drop-in replacement.

output_contract.note: Same shape as sections/statement/generate.json output_contract.schema — the pipeline treats this as a drop-in replacement for the failing draft.
